name: Spice Stock
intro: I usually make a batch of spice stock at the same time I make the base curry sauce. This isn’t one you really need to watch closely while cooking so it’s a good way of getting two jobs done at once. The aromatic strained stock can be added to curries when a little more liquid is desired to thin the sauce. So could water, for that matter, but this stock does give curries more depth.

ingredients.item: of green cardamom pods, lightly bruised
piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark
of coriander (cilantro), stems and leaves roughly chopped
or more fresh green chillies, to taste, halved lengthways (optional)

method: Put 1 litre (4¼ cups) of water in a pan and bring to a rolling boil. Throw in the herbs and spices and stir to combine. Reduce to a simmer and cook for about 30 minutes. Strain through a fine sieve and use immediately or store in airtight plastic containers in the fridge for up to 3 days. It also freezes well.
